# v1.0.2 release notes

GroundTruth OS can now update itself in place.

## What's new

- Added `Update OS` in the dashboard so the OS could update itself in place.
- Added an update-available indicator when a newer version was published.
- Changed startup so the dashboard opened to the Eisenhower task view instead of an empty welcome screen.

## Updating from older versions

If you were already running `v1.0.0` or `v1.0.1`, this was the first release where the updater path started to matter.

The historical release story told users they did not need to reinstall forever after this point, but older `v1.0.0` / `v1.0.1` systems still needed the one-time updater bootstrap described in the project docs before `Update OS` could take over.

## Historical notes for this release

- The visible install wording at this version still used `Install to disk`.
- The historical release story described this as a self-updating release and still carried the strong erase warning for the install path.

## Verify download

```sh
sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
```

You want to see `OK` next to the ISO name.
